title: CGAP Human Oesophagus Epithelium Dissociation
author: Adam Hunter
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qz8dx9w

steps.instruction: Receive oesophagus sample in solution.
Wash the samples with 10ml cold PBS to remove any residual contamination, stomach content and loose mucus.
Pour oesophagus onto 100mm glass petri dish and add another 10ml fresh cold PBS.
Open the samples longitudinally. Epithelium should be a relatively loose, yellowish layer on the lumen side.

Using two forceps separate the epithelium from stroma. Place them onto separate 100mm petri dishes each with 10ml PBS. From this stage on only process the epithelium. To process the mucosa/submucosa see protocol “”.
Aspirate PBS.
In a few drops of PBS (~200µl), finely mince the epithelium using two scalpels simultaneously. Too much PBS in the dish will make it more difficult to mince.
Add 4ml Trypsin-EDTA 0.25% to the dish and transfer the tissue to a 50ml falcon tube.
Wash the scalpel and the dish with a further 1ml of Trypsin-EDTA 0.25% and transfer it into the Falcon with the tissue.
Incubate the biopsies for 30 min at 37°C on a rocker.
Add 50µl DNAse I dropwise to falcon to a final concentration of 100µg/ml.
Incubate at room temperature for 5 min.
Add 20ml of RPMI + 20% FBS to inactivate trypsin.
Centrifuge at 200g for 2 minutes.
Pass the cells through 70µm cell strainer into 50ml falcon tube.
Wash the Falcon Tube and the strainer with 5ml of RPMI with 20% FBS for total volume of 30ml (5ml of trypsin + 25ml of media).
Centrifuge at 500g for 5 minutes at 4°C. Remove supernatant.
Wash the cells with 5ml Cold PBS.
Centrifuge at 500g for 5 minutes at 4°C.
Add 1ml Red Cell Lysis buffer to the pellet and resuspend by racking/tapping.
Place on ice 5 min with periodic agitation.
Add 10ml of Cold PBS.
Centrifuge at 500g for 5 minutes. Remove supernatant
Resuspend in 1ml cold PBS with 0.04% BSA (8µl BSA/ml PBS).
Filter through a 70µm cell strainer.
Count cells and viability using nucleocounter.

If percentage of live cells is higher than 70-80%, cells can then be processed for scRNA-seq. If percentage of live cells is below 70-80%, remove dead cells by following “Dead cell removal EasySep kit“. CGAP Dead cell removal EasySep kit with The Big Easy Magnet by Adam Hunter
Dilute cells to 2x10 6 cell per ml in 0.04% BSA and proceed to 10X Preparation for scRNA sequencing.

Ensure all unused tissue, equipment and tubes that have been in contact with primary tissue are placed into Virkon in sweetie jar for a minimum of 1 hour. After this time aspirate and disposing in relevant sharps or waste routes.
